report_name: North Korean Hackers Abuse GitHub for C2 in Campaign Targeting South Korea

threat_actors.name: Kimsuky
threat_actors.description: A North Korean state-sponsored threat actor involved in a campaign targeting South Korean organizations using LNK files and GitHub for C2.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and long-term intelligence gathering against South Korean targets.

threat_actors.name: Lazarus Group
threat_actors.description: A North Korean state-sponsored threat actor suspected to be involved in a campaign targeting South Korean organizations using LNK files and GitHub for C2.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and long-term intelligence gathering against South Korean targets.

threat_actors.name: APT37
threat_actors.description: A North Korean state-sponsored threat actor suspected to be involved in a campaign targeting South Korean organizations using LNK files and GitHub for C2.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and long-term intelligence gathering against South Korean targets.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Malicious Windows shortcut (.lnk) files, disguised with icons and names of common document types, were delivered via phishing to trick users into execution.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: A hidden PowerShell script was launched from a malicious LNK file to perform C2 communications and execute commands.

attack_patterns.name: Bidirectional Communication
attack_patterns.description: The malware abused the legitimate GitHub service as a C2 channel, posting stolen data to and pulling new commands from attacker-controlled repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malicious LNK file contained obfuscated commands that launched the PowerShell payload, and recent versions have shown increased obfuscation.

malware.name: PowerShell C2 Implant
malware.description: A PowerShell-based payload delivered by a malicious LNK file. It collects system information and uses GitHub for command and control, exfiltrating data and downloading further commands or payloads.

malware.name: XenoRAT
malware.description: A Remote Access Trojan (RAT) that can be delivered as a secondary payload via the GitHub C2 channel for long-term control and extensive data theft.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used as the execution engine for the primary malware payload, which communicates with the GitHub C2 and executes received commands.

identities.name: South Korean Organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations in South Korea targeted by a North Korean espionage campaign for political, military, and economic intelligence.

infrastructure.name: GitHub C2 Repositories
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled GitHub repositories used as a command-and-control channel. The malware posts stolen data to and pulls new commands from these repositories, camouflaging the traffic as legitimate web activity.

indicators.name: GitHub C2 Domain api.github.com
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Anomalous Process Connecting to GitHub API
id: 2b1d6a0f-8c7e-4b5a-9f1d-3c2e1b0a9f8d
status: experimental
description: Detects processes that are not common developer tools or browsers connecting to api.github.com, which could indicate abuse of GitHub for C2 communications.
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/github-backed-malware-spread-via-lnk-files-in-south-korea/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer tools, update clients, or scripts that interact with the GitHub API.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname: 'api.github.com'
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\git.exe'
      - '\gh.exe'
      - '\Code.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network connections to api.github.com from processes other than common developer tools or browsers. This activity is associated with the North Korean campaign using GitHub for C2.

indicators.name: PowerShell Execution from LNK File
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Execution from LNK File
id: 5e8f1c2a-3b4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d1e2f3a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell launched from a Windows shortcut (LNK) file, a technique used in a recent North Korean campaign.
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/github-backed-malware-spread-via-lnk-files-in-south-korea/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts launched via shortcuts, though this is uncommon.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '.lnk'
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell from a malicious LNK file, which was the initial execution vector in this campaign.

indicators.name: PowerShell GitHub C2 Script Block
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Script Making Web Request to GitHub API
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: 'Detects PowerShell script blocks (via Event ID 4104) containing web requests to api.github.com, indicating potential C2 activity.'
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/github-backed-malware-spread-via-lnk-files-in-south-korea/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or developer scripts that interact with the GitHub API.
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: powershell
  category: ps_script
detection:
  selection:
    ScriptBlockText|contains:
      - 'api.github.com'
      - 'Invoke-RestMethod'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
  condition: all of selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell script block logging events that show scripts making web requests to api.github.com, a key behavior of the C2 implant.
